active_alerts.description: Please see station personnel or use a station call box to request assistance.
To board: Use Kendall/MIT Elevator 866 located inside the station entrance.

Affected routes:
137

This elevator is closed for major equipment upgrades that will significantly improve its long-term reliability.
 
To board a train to Copley and West: Follow signs to the Red Line and take Park Street Elevator 808 to the Red Line platform. Then use Park Street Elevator 979 to the Green Line Copley and West platform.

The elevator is be closed for major equipment upgrades that will significantly improve the long-term reliability of this elevator.
 
To exit: Exit the train onto the center platform at Park Street Station. Travel to the south end of the platform and take Elevator 808 to the Winter St concourse, then elevator 804 to Winter St. 
 
To board: Take an Alewife train to Park Street Station (1 stop).  Exit train onto the center platform.  Transfer to an Ashmont or Braintree train.

Affected routes:
85
91
109

Please see station personnel or a train conductor for assistance. To exit, exit the train at Ruggles or Back Bay, then take a Forest Hills-bound train to Forest Hills and use Forest Hills Elevator 842. To board, use Forest Hills Elevator 842 to access the Orange Line platform, then take an Oak Grove-bound train to Ruggles or Back Bay and transfer to the Commuter Rail. Check the Commuter Rail train schedules for train arrival/departure times at Ruggles and Back Bay by visiting mbta.com/schedules.
Please see station personnel or use a station call box to request assistance.

There are accessible mini-high platforms at the western end of both the Track 1 and Track 2 sides. However, the existing pedestrian path between the two sides is long and includes steep slopes, especially on the Crest Road bridge over the tracks. Designs are underway to provide for a fully compliant pathway.

Accessibility:
All shuttle buses are accessible to riders with disabilities, however, the majority of buses used during weekdays will be lift-equipped, coach-style buses. Accessible van service will also be available on weekdays between Alewife and Park Street for those who prefer it – riders should see station personnel to request this service. The majority of shuttle bus service on weekends are low-floor, ramp-buses.

Express Shuttles Between Harvard and South Station:
Free and accessible express shuttles will be operating between Harvard (Eliot St @ Bennett St) and the South Station Bus Terminal (Gate 37). They will run every 15 minutes.

Alternative Bus Service:
Riders can use Bus Routes 69, 87, 88, 89, 91, and 109 from Davis Square, Harvard Square, and Central Square to connect with the Green Line’s Medford/Tufts and Union Square branches.

Alternate Rail Service:
Riders can use fare-free Commuter Rail service between Porter Sq and North Station. Regular fares should be purchased for travel beyond Porter. 

Travel Times:
Riders using shuttle bus service should budget ample extra travel time. A rider traveling between Alewife and Park Street should budget at least 70 minutes of travel time in addition to their regular commute.

Shuttle Locations:

Alewife Busway - Berths B1-4

Davis:
Northbound - Davis Busway
Southbound - Holland St @ Dover St​

Northbound - Massachusetts Ave @ Porter Station​

Please use nearby Downtown Crossing Elevator 998.

The elevator is closed for major equipment upgrades that will significantly improve the long-term reliability of this elevator.
 
To exit: Travel down the Summer St concourse towards the Red Line. About halfway down the concourse, take a left through the set of glass doors marked 101 Arch St. Follow signs to the elevator. Take elevator to the 101 Arch St lobby and exit on Summer St.
 
To board: Proceed to 101 Arch St on Summer St (across from Macy’s).  Take elevator located in the rear-left corner of the lobby to Summer St concourse.  At the concourse, turn right and follow signs to Oak Grove platform.
Scheduled to complete sometime in 2027, the reconstruction of Foster Street includes widening and resurfacing, with the addition of bicycle lanes.
To exit: Go to the east end of the platform. Take the ramp to Washington St. To board: Go east on North Ave for one block. Turn right on Washington St. Take the ramp to the Track 1 platform.

To exit: Go to the east end of the platform. Take the ramp to Washington St. To board: Go south on Main St. Turn left on South Ave and go one block. Turn left on Washington St. Take the ramp to the Track 2 platform.

To exit the Red Line, use nearby South Station Elevator 901. To board the Red Line, use South Station Elevator 901 located on the opposide side of the lobby. To board the Silver Line, take Elevator 901 to the Silver Line drop-off platform, then take South Station Elevator 927 to the Alewife platform. At the Alewife platform, use nearby South Station Elevator 919 to access the Airport/Design Center/Chelsea platform.

Alternate Service Options:
•	Riders who typically board or disembark at Symphony can instead board or disembark at Northeastern University or Prudential, which are 0.3 miles away (or about a five-minute walk) from Symphony. 
 
•	Riders are encouraged to instead consider travelling on the Orange Line at Massachusetts Avenue, which is 0.1 miles (or about a three-minute walk) from Symphony. 
 
•	Riders may also use the Route 39, which operates parallel to the E branch in both directions along Huntington Avenue. 

Background:
Currently, there are no step-free pathways from the entrances to the platforms at Symphony, and the station is not able to be accessed by people with wheeled mobility devices. When construction is complete in spring 2029, Symphony will be enhanced with four new accessible elevators, raised platforms for easier boarding, accessible bathrooms, improved wayfinding, more lighting, and upgraded safety features, making the station safer and easier to use for all riders.

Affected lines:
Haverhill Line

Regular weekday Franklin/Foxboro service is operating as scheduled throughout both days. The last train from Foxboro to South Station is scheduled to depart at 10:49pm.

Passengers can consider Providence/Stoughton Line service to Mansfield, which is just over 7 miles from the stadium.

Pedestrian Access:
There is no access between the Hingham Ferry Terminal and DCR building. Pedestrian access to and from the ferry dock is through and around the west side of the Hingham Ferry Terminal. Some sidewalk access at the Terminal may also be restricted during this work.

Shipyard Drive:
Shipyard Drive is closed to traffic in front of the Hingham Ferry Terminal. Traffic is being rerouted through the Hingham Parking lot. Drop-offs and pick-ups have been relocated to the parking lot. Traffic police and signage will be in place to direct traffic.

Affected lines:
Franklin/Foxboro Line

Skipped inbound stops:
Main St @ Essex St
Main St @ W Foster St - Melrose Sq
Main St @ Grove St

Beginning July 13, 2026, the Woburn Anderson Regional Transportation Center will undergo a major paving and infrastructure improvement project, with work expected to be completed by November 16, 2026. 

To support these critical renovations and enhance the long-term safety and functionality of the facility, select areas of the MBTA Daily Lot and the gated Overnight Lot will be temporarily closed throughout the construction period.

Motorists and facility users are encouraged to plan accordingly and carefully follow all posted signage, traffic control measures, and parking restrictions to ensure a safe and efficient experience during the project.

Skipped outbound stops:
Northern Ave @ Tide St

Skipped inbound stops:
Northern Ave @ Tide St

This elevator is closed for major equipment upgrades that will significantly improve its long-term reliability.
 
To board a northbound train: Proceed to the end of the platform and take Park Street Elevator 979 to the Red Line. At the end of the Red Line platform, use Elevator 808 to access the Winter St concourse and Green Line northbound platform.
Please use nearby ramp to the platform.

Affected lines:
Fall River/New Bedford Line

Skipped outbound stops:
Oxford St @ Washington St
Oxford St @ Market St

The escalator will remain out of service for the foreseeable future. This decision was made after a thorough inspection determined that corrosion on the escalator’s steel truss was so severe that it could not be repaired further and that a complete replacement of the escalator would be required. Replacing the escalator at this location requires a more extended unit meeting modern escalator safety requirements. This longer unit will require significant alteration to the station entrance to meet code requirements. The MBTA has currently begun design work to understand what station upgrades will be needed.

Due to these complexities, we expect the escalator will remain out of service for a significant time. We recognize the challenges this poses to our riders, and please know we are working to identify a solution as quickly as possible. In the meantime, Government Center Station, which is fully accessible and serves the Blue Line, is under a quarter-mile from Bowdoin Station.
